The queue/batch processor accepts spans, metrics, or logs and places them into
batches, using the [exporterhelper](../../exporter/exporterhelper) queue and
batch implementation. It is the replacement for the
[batchprocessor](../batchprocessor); see
[docs/rfcs/batching-migration.md](../../docs/rfcs/batching-migration.md).

Unlike the batch processor, the queue/batch processor:

- propagates export errors back to the caller by default (`wait_for_result: true`),
- applies backpressure when the queue is full by default (`block_on_overflow: true`),
- supports sizing batches by requests, items, or bytes,
- supports an optional persistent queue via a storage extension.

These defaults preserve the blocking, backpressure behavior that users relied on
from the batch processor.

Please refer to the [exporterhelper QueueBatchConfig](../../exporter/exporterhelper/queue_batch.go)
for the configuration spec.

## Internal telemetry

The exporterhelper's own queue and sender metrics are disabled (no
`otelcol_exporter_*` series are emitted). Instead this processor reports:

- `otelcol_processor_incoming_items` and `otelcol_processor_outgoing_items`,
  the standard processor item counters (matching `processorhelper`), counted as
  data arrives and as each batch is sent to the next consumer.
- `otelcol_processor_queuebatch_items` and `otelcol_processor_queuebatch_bytes`,
  histograms of the size of each emitted batch. These are collected only at the
  `detailed` telemetry level, because histograms are relatively expensive and
  the byte size is costly to compute.

All four metrics carry the `processor` (component ID) and `otel.signal`
attributes.

The processor also emits the exporterhelper's enqueue and export spans, but
reframed as processor spans: they are reported under this processor's
instrumentation scope, named `processor/enqueue` and
`processor/<id>/<signal>`, and carry the `processor` and `otel.signal`
attributes. When batching, the export span links to each batched request.
